input.headline: Will life insurance be worth it for seniors in 2026? The right life insurance policy (at the right price) could be valuable for seniors in 2026. Here's why.

raw.headline: Will life insurance be worth it for seniors in 2026? The right life insurance policy (at the right price) could be valuable for seniors in 2026. Here's why.

winner: Why will life insurance be a great investment for seniors in 2026? Because by then, they'll finally start charging you for all those years of living!

candidates.text: Why will senior citizens become professional musicians by 2026? Because they can now "compose" symphonies...with their life insurance policies!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and ties the topic to a humorous twist, but 'compose symphonies with life insurance policies' feels forced and lacks clear connection to the headline's focus on value and pricing."}

candidates.text: Why will seniors in 2026 be the best at life insurance sales? Because they can promise a payout even if you're still alive!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the topic, but the humor falls flat due to a slightly awkward punchline that doesn't fully land."}

candidates.text: Why did the senior cross the road in 2026? To get to the other side of a great life insurance policy!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat forced and lacks clear connection to the headline's content. It uses a classic 'why did the chicken cross the road' format but applies it awkwardly to life insurance, resulting in limited surprise and specificity."}

candidates.text: Why will life insurance be a great investment for seniors in 2026? Because by then, they'll finally start charging you for all those years of living!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the topic, but the humor feels forced and relies on a potentially offensive premise (charging for living). It lacks natural flow and could be seen as insensitive."}

candidates.text: Why did the senior cross the road in 2026? To prove he still had policy-curious legs!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'policy-curious legs,' but it's a bit forced and lacks strong surprise or specificity related to life insurance for seniors in 2026."}
